name: asynchronia
description: Project-specific automatic routing, Codex desktop UI-authoritative model preflight reconciliation, scope checks, and evidence workflows for every Codex task in the Asynchronia repository.

interface.shortDescription: Route tasks through UI-authoritative model inventory and scope preflight.
interface.longDescription: Provides mandatory routing, Codex desktop UI picker authority reconciled with codex app-server model/list catalog evidence, exhaustive evaluation of every authority-confirmed model-effort pair, adjacent-effort comparison, retry-adjusted cost selection, same-thread CONTINUE gating, scope isolation, deployment verification, and acceptance evidence rules.
defaultPrompt: Run codex app-server and call model/list with includeHidden false as supporting catalog evidence before recommending a model.
Reconcile app-server catalog evidence with the active Codex desktop UI picker before building candidates.
When app-server and UI differ, exact current same-thread UI inventory controls actual selectability.
Never add app-server-only models or efforts to a UI-authoritative candidate matrix.
Use each authority-confirmed model's supported efforts and evaluate every available authority-confirmed model-effort pair.
Compare each effort with its adjacent efforts for the same model and identify the lowest reliable effort per model.
Recommend the option that minimizes expected total credits after retry and escalation risk.
Explain why the next cheaper plausible pair is insufficient and why the next more expensive plausible pair is unnecessary.
Pause with WAITING_FOR_MODEL_SELECTION and wait for exact same-thread CONTINUE before any edits.
Use the repository skill source under plugins/asynchronia/skills/ as the mandatory fallback if needed.
Run task-router, scope-isolation-check, and model-selector before implementation.
